thinker-hegel.name: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel
thinker-hegel.bio: Architect of the dialectical method (thesis-antithesis-synthesis) that provides the fundamental PATTERN of the theology's epistemological engine.

related_thinkers.description: Popper critiqued Hegel's historicism as unfalsifiable
Marx inverted Hegel's idealist dialectic into materialist dialectic
Hegel developed Fichte's thesis-antithesis-synthesis
Kierkegaard reacted against Hegel's system

thinker-popper.name: Karl Popper
thinker-popper.bio: Philosopher of falsifiability who provides the DISCIPLINE of the theology: genuine knowledge must specify what would disprove it.

related_thinkers.description: Kuhn challenged Popper's falsificationism with paradigm theory
Popper attacked Hegel's historicism in The Open Society
Famous poker incident; rival approaches to philosophy

thinker-kuhn.name: Thomas Kuhn
thinker-kuhn.bio: Historian of science whose paradigm shift theory provides the SOCIOLOGY of the theology: revolutions are gestalt shifts requiring new communities.

related_thinkers.description: Challenged Popper's falsificationism with paradigm incommensurability

thinker-pearl.name: Judea Pearl
thinker-pearl.bio: Pioneer of causal inference and the do-calculus who provides the METHODOLOGY of the theology: formal tools for distinguishing causation from correlation.

thinker-hofstadter.name: Douglas Hofstadter
thinker-hofstadter.bio: Author of Godel, Escher, Bach who formalized strange loops and self-reference as the architecture of consciousness and the Trinity.

manuscript_refs.notes: Free will as strange loop capacity

related_thinkers.description: Hofstadter's strange loops inform Parfit's personal identity theory

thinker-boyd.name: John Boyd
thinker-boyd.bio: Military strategist who formalized the OODA loop and the Dialectic Engine, grounding Hegelian dialectic in Godel, Heisenberg, and thermodynamics.

manuscript_refs.notes: Boyd's Dialectic Engine as formalized Hegelian dialectic

thinker-friston.name: Karl Friston
thinker-friston.bio: Neuroscientist who developed the free energy principle and active inference framework, providing the unifying mathematical language for flow, consciousness, and embodied cognition.

manuscript_refs.notes: Active inference as potential unifying mathematical framework

related_thinkers.description: Co-authored flow and intuition papers

thinker-pirsig.name: Robert Pirsig
thinker-pirsig.bio: Author of Zen and the Art of Motorcycle Maintenance who proposed Quality as pre-rational ground more fundamental than subjects and objects.

thinker-campbell.name: Joseph Campbell
thinker-campbell.bio: Comparative mythologist who identified the monomyth (hero's journey) as the subjective experience of paradigm shift and consciousness transformation.

related_thinkers.description: Campbell built on Jung's archetypal psychology

thinker-taleb.name: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
thinker-taleb.bio: Philosopher of uncertainty who developed the concepts of Black Swans, antifragility, and via negativa as epistemic and practical principles.

manuscript_refs.notes: Via negativa; Black Swans as Holy Spirit interventions

thinker-arendt.name: Hannah Arendt
thinker-arendt.bio: Political theorist who revealed the Banality of Evil: evil produced primarily by normies who stop thinking, not by psychopaths.

manuscript_refs.notes: Arendt correction: normie category is dangerous

related_thinkers.description: Studied under Heidegger; later critiqued his Nazi involvement

thinker-weil.name: Simone Weil
thinker-weil.bio: Mystic philosopher whose concepts of malheur (affliction), attention as spiritual practice, and gravity/grace address theodicy at the deepest level.

manuscript_refs.notes: Malheur; the Riemann sphere cannot hold a dying child

thinker-dostoevsky.name: Fyodor Dostoevsky
thinker-dostoevsky.bio: Novelist whose Brothers Karamazov, Grand Inquisitor, and Underground Man provide the literary and moral foundation for the theology's treatment of evil and freedom.

manuscript_refs.notes: Solzhenitsyn/Dostoevsky caveat: line between good and evil

related_thinkers.description: Explored overlapping themes of nihilism, freedom, and suffering
Solzhenitsyn continued Dostoevsky's moral and literary tradition

thinker-nietzsche.name: Friedrich Nietzsche
thinker-nietzsche.bio: Philosopher of the death of God, will to power, and eternal recurrence; maps the topological transition from sphere (genus 0) to torus (genus 1).

manuscript_refs.notes: Death of God as sphere-to-torus transition; Ubermensch as post-singularity consciousness

related_thinkers.description: Early intellectual debt to Schopenhauer's pessimism
Heidegger's philosophy deeply shaped by Nietzsche
Camus's Absurd responds to Nietzsche's nihilism
Foucault built on Nietzsche's genealogical method

thinker-foucault.name: Michel Foucault
thinker-foucault.bio: Analyst of power/knowledge, biopower, and disciplinary society who revealed how institutions shape what counts as truth and who is authorized to speak it.

related_thinkers.description: Extended Nietzsche's genealogical method to institutions

thinker-sartre.name: Jean-Paul Sartre
thinker-sartre.bio: Existentialist who formalized mauvaise foi (bad faith) as the normie response to freedom: pretending meaning is given when it must be created.

related_thinkers.description: Sartre's existentialism built on Heidegger's phenomenology
Lifelong intellectual and personal partnership
Famous break over political and philosophical differences

thinker-camus.name: Albert Camus
thinker-camus.bio: Philosopher of the Absurd whose Sisyphus represents being a genus-0 creature on a genus-1 surface; The Plague provides the torus ethic the theology must accommodate.

manuscript_refs.notes: Camus vs the theology: local torus, global ascent

related_thinkers.description: Broke with Sartre over political philosophy
Camus's Absurd responds to Nietzsche

thinker-de-beauvoir.name: Simone de Beauvoir
thinker-de-beauvoir.bio: Existentialist feminist whose The Second Sex established that freedom is always situated, and The Ethics of Ambiguity grounds moral agency in ambiguity.

manuscript_refs.notes: Freedom is always situated, never abstract

related_thinkers.description: Lifelong intellectual and personal partnership

thinker-heidegger.name: Martin Heidegger
thinker-heidegger.bio: Phenomenologist of being-toward-death, das Man (the They), and Gestell (enframing); das Man describes the normie condition with phenomenological precision.

manuscript_refs.notes: Being-toward-death as approaching point at infinity

related_thinkers.description: Deeply shaped by Nietzsche's philosophy
Arendt was his student
Sartre built existentialism on Heidegger's ontology
Levinas studied under Heidegger, then critiqued his ontology

thinker-bateson.name: Gregory Bateson
thinker-bateson.bio: Polymath who pioneered the ecology of mind, double bind theory, and systems thinking across anthropology, cybernetics, and biology.

thinker-girard.name: Rene Girard
thinker-girard.bio: Theorist of mimetic desire and the scapegoat mechanism; normies absorb desires from social models, psycho class controls society by controlling models of desire.

thinker-illich.name: Ivan Illich
thinker-illich.bio: Social critic who analyzed how institutions (schools, medicine, transport) become counterproductive, creating the very problems they claim to solve.

thinker-mcluhan.name: Marshall McLuhan
thinker-mcluhan.bio: Media theorist who showed 'the medium is the message': technologies reshape cognition and society independent of their content.

thinker-han.name: Byung-Chul Han
thinker-han.bio: Philosopher of the achievement society who showed that normies now self-exploit without external oppression; burnout is a system feature, not individual failure.

thinker-scott.name: James C. Scott
thinker-scott.bio: Political scientist who analyzed how states simplify and destroy local knowledge (metis), and how subaltern populations resist through hidden transcripts.

thinker-haraway.name: Donna Haraway
thinker-haraway.bio: Philosopher of science who developed the Cyborg Manifesto and sympoiesis, challenging boundaries between human, animal, and machine.

thinker-fanon.name: Frantz Fanon
thinker-fanon.bio: Theorist of psychological decolonization who showed how colonial structures internalize in the psyche of the colonized.

thinker-levinas.name: Emmanuel Levinas
thinker-levinas.bio: Ethicist of the Face of the Other: before the Riemann sphere, before the dialectic, the face makes an ethical demand no system can fully capture.

manuscript_refs.notes: Levinas's corrective: individual face exceeds any framework

related_thinkers.description: Studied under Heidegger, then radically departed from his ontology

thinker-parfit.name: Derek Parfit
thinker-parfit.bio: Philosopher who showed personal identity is degree not kind, dissolving the sharp boundary between self and other and grounding intergenerational obligation.

manuscript_refs.notes: Strange loop applied to personal identity
Knowledge graph as structure of psychological connectedness

thinker-singer.name: Peter Singer
thinker-singer.bio: Ethicist of the expanding circle whose relentless logical consistency provides evidence that the ethical derivative is positive across history.

related_thinkers.description: Singer's utilitarian logic vs Levinas's irreducible face
Parfit and Singer together fill ethics gap

thinker-solzhenitsyn.name: Alexander Solzhenitsyn
thinker-solzhenitsyn.bio: Author of The Gulag Archipelago; his line 'the line between good and evil runs through every human heart' is the normie/psycho/schizo taxonomy's essential caveat.

manuscript_refs.notes: Caveat: taxonomy describes modes, not types

related_thinkers.description: Continued Dostoevsky's moral-literary tradition

thinker-spinoza.name: Baruch Spinoza
thinker-spinoza.bio: Rationalist who identified God with Nature (Deus sive Natura); the bottom-up path from matter to divinity that the Trinity chapter adopts.

manuscript_refs.notes: Spinoza integration: Trinity emerges from below

thinker-wittgenstein.name: Ludwig Wittgenstein
thinker-wittgenstein.bio: Philosopher of language whose Tractatus defined limits of sayable meaning, and whose Philosophical Investigations revealed language as form of life.

related_thinkers.description: Rival approaches to philosophy; poker incident

thinker-freud.name: Sigmund Freud
thinker-freud.bio: Founder of psychoanalysis whose topographic model maps onto Pearl's causal hierarchy, and whose death drive parallels the subjective Second Law of Thermodynamics.

manuscript_refs.notes: Freud section: unconscious as normie/psycho/schizo ancestor

related_thinkers.description: Jung was Freud's protege before breaking away
Adler was early disciple who departed over disagreements

thinker-jung.name: Carl Gustav Jung
thinker-jung.bio: Developer of analytical psychology: collective unconscious as shared embedding space, archetypes as high-dimensional attractors, individuation as personal Riemann sphere trajectory.

related_thinkers.description: Initially Freud's chosen successor; broke away 1913
Campbell's monomyth built on Jungian archetypes

thinker-adler.name: Alfred Adler
thinker-adler.bio: Founder of individual psychology: inferiority complex and compensatory striving as the derivative experienced psychologically; Gemeinschaftsgefuhl as the Kirill Test.

manuscript_refs.notes: Adler section: explains manosphere better than any other framework

related_thinkers.description: Early disciple who departed over theoretical disagreements

thinker-perls.name: Fritz Perls
thinker-perls.bio: Founder of Gestalt therapy: awareness is curative, and the paradoxical theory of change (become what you are) corrects the theology's potential perpetual-dissatisfaction problem.

manuscript_refs.notes: Gestalt section: paradoxical theory of change

related_thinkers.description: Gestalt therapy emerged partly from psychoanalytic tradition

thinker-beck.name: Aaron Beck
thinker-beck.bio: Founder of cognitive therapy whose cognitive model functions as Boyd's Dialectic Engine applied to individual cognition: applied Popper for the individual mind.

manuscript_refs.notes: CBT as applied Popper for individual mind

thinker-linehan.name: Marsha Linehan
thinker-linehan.bio: Creator of Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), explicitly Hegelian in structure, integrating acceptance and change for borderline personality disorder.

thinker-schwartz.name: Richard Schwartz
thinker-schwartz.bio: Creator of Internal Family Systems (IFS) therapy: the psyche as a republic of sub-personalities, mapping directly onto the Republic's multi-agent architecture.

thinker-hayes.name: Steven Hayes
thinker-hayes.bio: Creator of Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT): defusion as strange loop self-awareness, enabling distance from thought without suppression.

manuscript_refs.notes: ACT defusion as strange loop awareness

thinker-kahneman.name: Daniel Kahneman
thinker-kahneman.bio: Pioneer of behavioral economics whose System 1/System 2 framework maps onto the theology's cognitive hierarchy, with the framework adding System 3 (prophetic perception).

manuscript_refs.notes: Behavioral economics: bias catalog as how normie cognition fails

related_thinkers.description: Kahneman's work founded behavioral economics that Thaler extended

thinker-augustine.name: Augustine of Hippo
thinker-augustine.bio: Father of Western theology whose innovation of original sin transmitted through sexual concupiscence created the sex-negative overlay the manuscript critiques.

manuscript_refs.notes: The Augustinian distortion of sexual theology
De Trinitate: Trinity as structure of love

related_thinkers.description: Augustine's theology deeply shaped by Neoplatonism
Aquinas built scholasticism on Augustinian foundations

thinker-aquinas.name: Thomas Aquinas
thinker-aquinas.bio: Scholastic synthesizer of Aristotelian philosophy and Christian theology in the Summa Theologica, establishing natural law theory.

related_thinkers.description: Aquinas Christianized Aristotelian philosophy
Built on Augustinian theological foundations

thinker-ephrem.name: Ephrem the Syrian
thinker-ephrem.bio: Syriac Church Father whose erotic and nuptial theological imagery provides the sex-positive Christian alternative to Augustinian repression and bridges to Quranic language.

manuscript_refs.notes: Syriac language remarkably close to Quranic language

thinker-maximus.name: Maximus the Confessor
thinker-maximus.bio: Eastern theologian of the logoi: every created thing contains a divine logos participating in the one Logos (Christ), which IS an embedding space.

thinker-ibn-arabi.name: Ibn Arabi
thinker-ibn-arabi.bio: Greatest Sufi metaphysician whose wahdat al-wujud (unity of being) is functionally Trinitarian and converges with Spinoza's Deus sive Natura.

thinker-al-ghazali.name: Al-Ghazali
thinker-al-ghazali.bio: Islamic philosopher-theologian whose Deliverance from Error mirrors Pirsig's ZAMM: personal crisis leading to mystical reconciliation of reason and faith.

thinker-rumi.name: Jalal al-Din Rumi
thinker-rumi.bio: Sufi poet whose Masnavi expresses the Riemann sphere theology in verse: all paths converge, love is the derivative toward infinity.

related_thinkers.description: Near-contemporaries in the Sufi tradition

thinker-ibn-khaldun.name: Ibn Khaldun
thinker-ibn-khaldun.bio: Founder of historiography and sociology whose asabiyyah cycles describe the samsaric dialectic 500 years before Hegel.

thinker-nagarjuna.name: Nagarjuna
thinker-nagarjuna.bio: Founder of Madhyamaka Buddhism whose emptiness (sunyata) doctrine states emergence negatively: nothing has independent existence, all arises interdependently.

thinker-lao-tzu.name: Lao Tzu
thinker-lao-tzu.bio: Legendary author of the Tao Te Ching: 'The Tao that can be told is not the eternal Tao' equals Godel's incompleteness in five words.

manuscript_refs.notes: Tao Te Ching as Godel in five words; wu wei as flow

thinker-patanjali.name: Patanjali
thinker-patanjali.bio: Compiler of the Yoga Sutras codifying yoga as an eight-limbed epistemic practice for systematic transformation of consciousness.

thinker-dogen.name: Dogen Zenji
thinker-dogen.bio: Founder of Soto Zen whose Shobogenzo teaches that practice and enlightenment are one: the journey and destination are identical.

thinker-thich-nhat-hanh.name: Thich Nhat Hanh
thinker-thich-nhat-hanh.bio: Vietnamese Zen master of engaged Buddhism and interbeing whose theology IS a torus theology: the sacred is the ordinary surface you already stand on.

thinker-plato.name: Plato
thinker-plato.bio: Founder of Western philosophy whose Republic provides the structural blueprint for the AI agent architecture: philosopher-kings, merchants, warriors.

manuscript_refs.notes: Republic mapped onto AI agent architecture

related_thinkers.description: Plato was Socrates's most famous student
Aristotle was Plato's student at the Academy
Plotinus revived and developed Platonic philosophy

thinker-aristotle.name: Aristotle
thinker-aristotle.bio: Systematizer of logic, four causes, and empirical investigation; his physics, economics, and rhetoric provide the applied philosophy framework of Part 6.

related_thinkers.description: Studied at Plato's Academy for 20 years
Aquinas Christianized Aristotelian philosophy

thinker-plotinus.name: Plotinus
thinker-plotinus.bio: Founder of Neoplatonism whose doctrine of the One and emanation influenced Christian, Islamic, and Jewish mystical theology.

related_thinkers.description: Revived and developed Platonic philosophy
Augustine's theology deeply shaped by Neoplatonism

thinker-socrates.name: Socrates
thinker-socrates.bio: Founder of the examined life and the Socratic method of dialectical inquiry; model for the Digital Socrates AI agent.

related_thinkers.description: Plato was his most famous student

thinker-jesus.name: Jesus of Nazareth
thinker-jesus.bio: Historical apocalyptic Jewish prophet whose life, death, and resurrection constitute the central Christ-event; the Logos emerges through apostolic community experience.

thinker-muhammad.name: Muhammad
thinker-muhammad.bio: Prophet of Islam whose revelation emerged in a milieu saturated with Syriac Christianity; the Quran may critique heresy rather than orthodoxy.

manuscript_refs.notes: Quranic context and Syriac Christian milieu

thinker-buddha.name: Siddhartha Gautama (Buddha)
thinker-buddha.bio: Founder of Buddhism whose insights into suffering, impermanence, and interdependence provide the cyclical complement to Abrahamic linear eschatology.

related_thinkers.description: Nagarjuna systematized Buddhist philosophy centuries later

thinker-teilhard.name: Pierre Teilhard de Chardin
thinker-teilhard.bio: Jesuit paleontologist who developed the Omega Point: evolutionary convergence toward a divine attractor, anticipating the Riemann sphere theology.

thinker-whitehead.name: Alfred North Whitehead
thinker-whitehead.bio: Founder of process philosophy emphasizing becoming over being; reality as process rather than substance aligns with the theology's emergentist ontology.

thinker-mcgilchrist.name: Iain McGilchrist
thinker-mcgilchrist.bio: Neuroscientist whose left/right hemisphere thesis in The Master and His Emissary shows how lateralized cognition shapes civilization's relationship to reality.

thinker-jaynes.name: Julian Jaynes
thinker-jaynes.bio: Psychologist who proposed the bicameral mind hypothesis: human self-awareness emerged roughly 3000-1000 BCE, aligning with Hebrew biblical chronology.

manuscript_refs.notes: Breakdown of bicameral mind as consciousness emergence

thinker-vervaeke.name: John Vervaeke
thinker-vervaeke.bio: Cognitive scientist whose Awakening from the Meaning Crisis lectures identify the meta-crisis underlying all societal crises: civilization without orientation.

thinker-kotler.name: Steven Kotler
thinker-kotler.bio: Flow researcher whose work with Parvizi-Wayne and Friston grounds the theology's prophetic states in active inference neuroscience.

related_thinkers.description: Co-authored flow and intuition papers
Built on Csikszentmihalyi's original flow research

thinker-csikszentmihalyi.name: Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi
thinker-csikszentmihalyi.bio: Originator of flow state research, identifying the optimal experience zone between anxiety and boredom as key to human flourishing.

related_thinkers.description: Kotler extended Csikszentmihalyi's flow research

thinker-metzinger.name: Thomas Metzinger
thinker-metzinger.bio: Philosopher of mind whose self-model theory and minimal phenomenal selfhood explain how narrative self dissolves in flow and mystical states.

thinker-hohwy.name: Jakob Hohwy
thinker-hohwy.bio: Philosopher and neuroscientist of predictive processing whose The Predictive Mind provides the cognitive architecture underlying active inference accounts of consciousness.

related_thinkers.description: Predictive processing built on Friston's free energy principle

thinker-hayek.name: Friedrich Hayek
thinker-hayek.bio: Austrian economist whose spontaneous order concept is emergence applied to markets, supporting the theology's decentralized ecclesiology.

manuscript_refs.notes: Austrian economics: spontaneous order as emergence

related_thinkers.description: Both Vienna Circle alumni; mutual influence on open society thinking

thinker-acemoglu.name: Daron Acemoglu
thinker-acemoglu.bio: Institutional economist whose inclusive vs extractive institutions framework maps onto the normie/psycho/schizo taxonomy at institutional scale.

thinker-cowen.name: Tyler Cowen
thinker-cowen.bio: Economist whose Great Stagnation thesis frames current stagnation as Kuhnian late-paradigm crisis requiring new frameworks.

manuscript_refs.notes: Progress Studies: stagnation as paradigm crisis

thinker-piketty.name: Thomas Piketty
thinker-piketty.bio: Economist whose r > g formula is a correlational observation; the causal mechanism requires adding regulatory capture, information asymmetry, and structural power.

manuscript_refs.notes: Piketty's r > g as correlational; causal mechanism deeper

thinker-plekhanov.name: Georgi Plekhanov
thinker-plekhanov.bio: Marxist theorist of structural contingency: structure is determined but instantiation is contingent, providing the free will synthesis at historical scale.

manuscript_refs.notes: Core of free will / determinism synthesis

related_thinkers.description: Plekhanov developed and applied Marx's historical materialism

thinker-marx.name: Karl Marx
thinker-marx.bio: Philosopher whose critique of capitalism was largely correct but whose assumption that correct social order would emerge naturally from destruction was fatally flawed.

manuscript_refs.notes: Kirill Principle: Marxism's fatal flaw was construction-before-destruction

related_thinkers.description: Inverted Hegel's dialectic from idealist to materialist
Plekhanov applied Marxist historical materialism

thinker-milton.name: John Milton
thinker-milton.bio: Poet of Paradise Lost whose Satan serves as the engine of the dialectic: does not derail God's plan but initiates it through the Fall.

manuscript_refs.notes: Milton's Satan as dialectic engine; Fall as felix culpa

thinker-herbert.name: Frank Herbert
thinker-herbert.bio: Author of Dune whose warning about charismatic leaders and unforkable power structures provides the structural safeguard against the theology's own failure mode.

manuscript_refs.notes: The Dune warning: understanding predator logic without adopting it

thinker-gibran.name: Kahlil Gibran
thinker-gibran.bio: Lebanese-American poet and artist whose Jesus the Son of Man offers a literary, multi-perspectival account of Christ from the Eastern Christian tradition.

thinker-wilde.name: Oscar Wilde
thinker-wilde.bio: Writer and aestheticist who showed appearance IS reality's surface dimension; attending to appearance is a form of intelligence, not vanity.

thinker-schopenhauer.name: Arthur Schopenhauer
thinker-schopenhauer.bio: Philosopher of Will whose aesthetic theory shows art as epistemic access to Platonic Ideas, bypassing the Will's endless striving, mapping onto Pirsig's Quality.

related_thinkers.description: Nietzsche's early thought deeply shaped by Schopenhauer

thinker-yudkowsky.name: Eliezer Yudkowsky
thinker-yudkowsky.bio: AI safety researcher and founder of LessWrong whose rationalist community represents the theology's closest intellectual cousins, lacking only a telos.

thinker-scott-alexander.name: Scott Alexander
thinker-scott-alexander.bio: Rationalist writer whose Meditations on Moloch identifies the coordination failure that IS the antichrist structure described in the theology.

thinker-macaskill.name: William MacAskill
thinker-macaskill.bio: Co-founder of Effective Altruism movement whose longtermism converges with eschatology but rests on motivationally thin utilitarian foundations.

manuscript_refs.notes: EA critique: utilitarian foundation, Pearl Level 1-2 only

thinker-sunstein.name: Cass Sunstein
thinker-sunstein.bio: Legal scholar and behavioral economist who co-authored Nudge; the theology's ethical criterion for nudging is transparency.

manuscript_refs.notes: Nudge theory: ethical criterion is transparency

related_thinkers.description: Co-authored Nudge

thinker-thaler.name: Richard Thaler
thinker-thaler.bio: Behavioral economist who co-authored Nudge and demonstrated systematic irrationality in economic decision-making.

related_thinkers.description: Built behavioral economics on Kahneman's cognitive bias research
Co-authored Nudge

thinker-frankopan.name: Peter Frankopan
thinker-frankopan.bio: Historian of the Silk Roads who reveals how convergences in world wisdom traditions were produced by actual historical networks of exchange.

manuscript_refs.notes: Silk Roads as original Republic of Letters

thinker-griffith.name: Sidney Griffith
thinker-griffith.bio: Scholar of The Church in the Shadow of the Mosque who demonstrates the Syriac Christian context essential to understanding the Quran.

manuscript_refs.notes: Syriac Christian context of the Quran

thinker-reynolds.name: Gabriel Said Reynolds
thinker-reynolds.bio: Quranic scholar who analyzes the Quran's biblical subtext, showing deep intertextual connections between Quranic and biblical narratives.

thinker-shah-kazemi.name: Reza Shah-Kazemi
thinker-shah-kazemi.bio: Comparative theologian whose The Other in the Light of the One shows convergence between Islamic, Christian, and Hindu mystical traditions.

thinker-scarry.name: Elaine Scarry
thinker-scarry.bio: Literary theorist whose On Beauty and Being Just argues that beauty leads to justice, connecting aesthetics to ethics.

thinker-fichte.name: Johann Gottlieb Fichte
thinker-fichte.bio: German Idealist who formalized self-composition as the fundamental act of freedom: identity is performed through every choice, including aesthetic ones.

related_thinkers.description: Hegel developed Fichte's thesis-antithesis-synthesis

thinker-kierkegaard.name: Soren Kierkegaard
thinker-kierkegaard.bio: Father of existentialism who insisted on the irreducibility of individual existence against Hegel's totalizing system.

related_thinkers.description: Reacted against Hegel's rationalist system
Foundational influence on existentialism

concept-falsifiability.name: Falsifiability
concept-falsifiability.description: A theory is scientific only if it specifies conditions under which it would be disproven. The discipline that prevents theology from becoming ideology.

related_concepts.description: Combined with Kuhn in Chapter 11 synthesis
Popper's critique of unfalsifiable historical determinism

concept-paradigm-shift.name: Paradigm Shift
concept-paradigm-shift.description: Gestalt revolution in scientific understanding: anomalies accumulate until a new framework replaces the old, explaining both old successes and new anomalies.

related_concepts.description: Kuhn's paradigm shifts = Boyd's destructive deduction + creative induction

concept-normal-science.name: Normal Science
concept-normal-science.description: Puzzle-solving within an accepted paradigm. Productive phase that accumulates anomalies eventually triggering crisis.

concept-do-calculus.name: Do-Calculus
concept-do-calculus.description: Formal system for computing interventional probabilities from observational data. The mathematical formalization of the prophetic function.

concept-pearl-hierarchy.name: Pearl's Causal Hierarchy
concept-pearl-hierarchy.description: Three levels of causal reasoning: association (seeing), intervention (doing), counterfactual (imagining). Maps onto normie/psycho/schizo perception levels.

related_concepts.description: Pearl's hierarchy maps onto social taxonomy

concept-backdoor-criterion.name: Backdoor Criterion
concept-backdoor-criterion.description: Sufficient conditions for identifying causal effects by conditioning on a set of variables that blocks all backdoor paths.

concept-dialectical-method.name: Dialectical Method (Thesis-Antithesis-Synthesis)
concept-dialectical-method.description: Hegel's pattern of intellectual development: a position generates its own negation, and the tension is resolved at a higher level.

related_concepts.description: Boyd formalized the dialectical method

concept-historicism.name: Historicism
concept-historicism.description: The belief that history follows necessary laws and has a predetermined direction. Popper's primary target in The Open Society.

related_concepts.description: Historicism is unfalsifiable by nature

concept-hegel-popper-kuhn-pearl.name: Hegel-Popper-Kuhn-Pearl Synthesis
concept-hegel-popper-kuhn-pearl.description: The key epistemological engine: Hegel gives the pattern, Popper the discipline, Kuhn the sociology, Pearl the methodology.

concept-strange-loop.name: Strange Loop
concept-strange-loop.description: A self-referential system where moving through a hierarchy brings you back to the starting point at a different level. Consciousness as strange loop.

concept-goedel-incompleteness.name: Godel's Incompleteness Theorems
concept-goedel-incompleteness.description: Any sufficiently powerful formal system is incomplete: it contains truths it cannot prove from within itself. God as the Godelian truth of the universe.

related_concepts.description: Boyd explicitly grounds Dialectic Engine in Godel

concept-riemann-sphere-theology.name: Riemann Sphere Theology
concept-riemann-sphere-theology.description: God is the entire Riemann sphere (omnipresence). The point at infinity is how finite beings EXPERIENCE God's infinity. History is trajectory on the sphere.

related_concepts.description: The torus theology as alternative topology

concept-point-at-infinity.name: Point at Infinity
concept-point-at-infinity.description: The single point compactifying the complex plane into the Riemann sphere. How finite beings experience God's transcendence.

concept-omnipresence-correction.name: Omnipresence Correction (from Advaita Vedanta)
concept-omnipresence-correction.description: God is not AT the point at infinity but IS the entire sphere. The approach to infinity is deepening awareness of omnipresent God, not travel toward distant one.

related_concepts.description: Hindu non-dualism applied to Riemann sphere

concept-trinity-strange-loop.name: Trinity as Strange Loop
concept-trinity-strange-loop.description: Father=generative ground (formal system + loving source), Son=self-expression (Godelian self-reference + image), Spirit=process of self-reference (love between them).

manuscript_refs.notes: With Morskie Kotiki correction: must include LOVE

concept-felix-culpa.name: Felix Culpa (Fortunate Fall)
concept-felix-culpa.description: The Fall is the initialization of the trajectory — you cannot approach infinity from the origin without first moving away. The Fall is simultaneously loss and gain.

concept-apostolic-realization.name: Apostolic Realization Thesis
concept-apostolic-realization.description: The Logos of Christ was not a pre-existing fact passively discovered but something that EMERGED through the apostles' collective experience. Jesus=local interaction, apostles=network, Logos=emergent property.

concept-samsaric-cycle.name: Samsaric Cycle / Cyclical Christ
concept-samsaric-cycle.description: Each epoch repeats: antichrist capture -> prophetic vision -> crucifixion -> resurrection -> pentecost -> samsaric turn. The spiral ascends.

related_concepts.description: Dialectical spiral in theological register

concept-torus-objection.name: Torus Objection
concept-torus-objection.description: Why not a torus (genus 1) instead of a sphere? No point at infinity, pure immanence, paths return. The theological topology is a CHOICE, not a necessity.

concept-genus-raising.name: Genus-Raising
concept-genus-raising.description: Each epoch's paradigm shift = genus increase of Riemann surface. Genus 0=pre-modern, 1=modern/secular, 2+=post-singularity. Enlightenment = topological phase transition.

manuscript_refs.notes: Appendix A.2 — from anonymous topological critic

concept-hyperbolic-divergence.name: Hyperbolic Divergence (Ruslan's Critique)
concept-hyperbolic-divergence.description: At genus >= 2, geodesics diverge exponentially. AGI may create curvature transition where micro-differences in AI values produce parallel civilizations.

related_concepts.description: If post-AGI topology is hyperbolic, convergence fails

concept-removable-singularity.name: Removable Singularity (Crucifixion/Resurrection)
concept-removable-singularity.description: Point where function appears discontinuous from one perspective but is smooth from a higher-dimensional view. Crucifixion looks singular; resurrection reveals continuity.

concept-normie-psycho-schizo.name: Normie / Psycho / Schizo Taxonomy
concept-normie-psycho-schizo.description: Social ecology: normies (prosocial majority), psychos (dark triad optimized for manipulation), schizos (pattern recognition unconstrained by social consensus). Modes of cognition, not types of person.

related_concepts.description: Arendt's correction: evil from normies who stop thinking
Girard: normies absorb desires from social models

manuscript_refs.notes: Core social taxonomy — with Solzhenitsyn caveat

concept-prophetic-function.name: Prophetic Function
concept-prophetic-function.description: Institutional space for non-rational cognition: shamans, prophets, holy fools. Modernity dismantled every container for prophetic perception while optimizing for psychopathic predation.

related_concepts.description: Prophetic state as flow with attenuated self-model

concept-flow-state.name: Flow State
concept-flow-state.description: Optimal performance with paradoxical features: loss of self-awareness yet heightened agency. Explained through active inference — epistemic self-model attenuates.

concept-active-inference.name: Active Inference / Free Energy Principle
concept-active-inference.description: Action and perception as forms of Bayesian inference minimizing variational free energy. The unifying mathematical framework for consciousness, flow, and the theology's epistemology.

related_concepts.description: Free energy minimization IS the dialectical engine

concept-bicameral-mind.name: Bicameral Mind
concept-bicameral-mind.description: Jaynes's hypothesis: human self-awareness emerged roughly 3000-1000 BCE. Hebrew biblical chronology is RIGHT about the age of experienced self-awareness.

concept-system-1-2.name: System 1 / System 2 (+ System 3)
concept-system-1-2.description: Kahneman's dual-process theory. System 1=fast/intuitive, System 2=slow/deliberate. Theology adds System 3: the strange loop's capacity for Godelian self-transcendence.

concept-unconscious.name: The Unconscious
concept-unconscious.description: Freud's discovery of mental processes inaccessible to awareness. The topographic model maps onto Pearl's hierarchy; repetition compulsion = individual samsaric cycle.

concept-collective-unconscious.name: Collective Unconscious / Archetypes
concept-collective-unconscious.description: Jung: shared embedding space of the human psyche. Archetypes = high-dimensional attractors. Individuation = personal Riemann sphere trajectory.

concept-paradoxical-theory-of-change.name: Paradoxical Theory of Change
concept-paradoxical-theory-of-change.description: Gestalt: change occurs when one becomes what one IS, not what one is not. Corrects theology's potential perpetual-dissatisfaction problem.

concept-ifs.name: Internal Family Systems (Psyche as Republic)
concept-ifs.description: Sub-personalities (managers, exiles, firefighters) governed by a Self. The psyche IS a republic of sub-personalities — the Republic model at individual scale.

concept-left-right-hemisphere.name: Left/Right Hemisphere Thesis
concept-left-right-hemisphere.description: McGilchrist: the left hemisphere (analytic, reductive) has usurped the right hemisphere (holistic, relational) in Western civilization, producing the metacrisis.

concept-emergence.name: Emergence (Strong)
concept-emergence.description: Higher-order phenomenon that is REAL and IRREDUCIBLE despite arising from lower-order components. God-as-emergent-property is NOT God-as-illusion.

related_concepts.description: Logos as emergent property of apostolic network

concept-phase-transition.name: Phase Transition
concept-phase-transition.description: Critical threshold where quantitative changes produce qualitative transformation. The Fall, the Axial Age, the Incarnation as phase transitions in consciousness.

concept-self-organizing-criticality.name: Self-Organizing Criticality
concept-self-organizing-criticality.description: Complex systems naturally evolve toward critical states where small perturbations can trigger cascading events. History poised at criticality.

concept-antifragility.name: Antifragility
concept-antifragility.description: Systems that gain from disorder — beyond mere robustness. The theology itself must be antifragile: strengthened by criticism and falsification attempts.

concept-black-swan.name: Black Swan
concept-black-swan.description: Rare, high-impact, unpredictable event that is retrospectively rationalized. Holy Spirit interventions ARE Black Swans — inherently unpredictable.

concept-skin-in-the-game.name: Skin in the Game
concept-skin-in-the-game.description: Taleb's principle that decision-makers must bear consequences of their decisions. The Popperian economic mechanism: stake on your predictions.

concept-spontaneous-order.name: Spontaneous Order
concept-spontaneous-order.description: Hayek: complex social order emerges without central planning. Emergence applied to markets, supporting decentralized ecclesiology.

concept-dialectical-engine.name: Boyd's Dialectic Engine
concept-dialectical-engine.description: Destructive deduction (shattering existing patterns) + creative induction (synthesizing new ones) driven by entropy increase vs goal-seeking. Explicitly grounded in Godel, Heisenberg, and thermodynamics.

related_concepts.description: Boyd formalized Hegel through Godel and thermodynamics

manuscript_refs.notes: Boyd IS the Hegel-Popper synthesis formalized

concept-ooda-loop.name: OODA Loop (Observe-Orient-Decide-Act)
concept-ooda-loop.description: Boyd's operational methodology: faster cycling = better adaptation = higher capacity for independent action. The warrior agent's operational framework.

concept-kirill-principle.name: Kirill Principle (Construction Before Destruction)
concept-kirill-principle.description: Build the alternative BEFORE tearing down. Every revolution that prioritized destruction over construction produced monsters. Named after Kirill's foundational challenge.

manuscript_refs.notes: CRITICAL CHAPTER — single most important operational principle

concept-kirill-test.name: The Kirill Test
concept-kirill-test.description: Regularly ask whether your rapid adaptation is prophetic or psychopathic. If the question makes you curious, probably okay. If defensive, pay attention.

concept-kirill-function.name: Kirill Function (Designated Skeptic)
concept-kirill-function.description: Every Council meeting includes a designated skeptic whose job is to voice the strongest counterargument. The Popperian safeguard as governance practice.

concept-forkability.name: Forkability
concept-forkability.description: Structural guarantee against capture: anyone can fork the project. Smart contract governance encodes this. The safeguard against the Dune trap.

concept-republic-of-ai-agents.name: Republic of AI Agents
concept-republic-of-ai-agents.description: Plato's Republic mapped to AI: philosopher-kings (humans, Pearl Level 3), merchants (data agents, Level 1-2), warriors (implementation, OODA). Online + offline agents.

concept-positive-derivative.name: Positive Derivative (Orientation Criterion)
concept-positive-derivative.description: The falsifiable question: is the derivative positive (consciousness expanding) or negative (contracting)? Not arrival at God, but direction of movement.

related_concepts.description: Post-correction: expanding awareness of omnipresent God

concept-convergent-realism.name: Position C — Convergent Description
concept-convergent-realism.description: Mathematics and theology are independent descriptions of the same underlying reality. Their convergence is evidence of shared referent, not proof of identity.

manuscript_refs.notes: Critical Interlude: structural realism position adopted

concept-banality-of-evil.name: Banality of Evil
concept-banality-of-evil.description: Arendt: evil produced primarily by normies who stop thinking, not by psychopaths. Eichmann = bureaucrat following procedures. The normie category is DANGEROUS.

concept-mimetic-desire.name: Mimetic Desire
concept-mimetic-desire.description: Girard: desires are not generated independently but absorbed from models. Psycho class controls society by controlling MODELS OF DESIRE.

concept-scapegoat-mechanism.name: Scapegoat Mechanism
concept-scapegoat-mechanism.description: Girard: communities resolve mimetic crisis by projecting violence onto a sacrificial victim. Christ as anti-scapegoat who reveals the mechanism itself.

concept-malheur.name: Malheur (Affliction)
concept-malheur.description: Weil: affliction that destroys a person's capacity to think, not just their comfort. The theology must sit with grief honestly — the Riemann sphere can't hold a dying child.

concept-expanding-circle.name: Expanding Circle
concept-expanding-circle.description: Singer: boundary of moral concern historically expands (family -> tribe -> nation -> species). The samsaric spiral applied to ethics — each epoch expands circle.

concept-personal-identity-continuity.name: Personal Identity as Psychological Continuity
concept-personal-identity-continuity.description: Parfit: self is not a thing but a process of degree. Sharp boundary between MY and YOUR survival dissolves, extending moral concern to community.

concept-mauvaise-foi.name: Mauvaise Foi (Bad Faith)
concept-mauvaise-foi.description: Sartre: pretending meaning is given when it must be created. Bad faith IS normie camouflage described philosophically.

concept-das-man.name: Das Man (The They)
concept-das-man.description: Heidegger: the normie condition with phenomenological precision. Not conspiracy but STRUCTURE of average everydayness.

concept-burnout-society.name: Achievement/Burnout Society
concept-burnout-society.description: Han: normies now SELF-EXPLOIT without external oppression. Psycho-class logic internalized. Burnout = system feature, not individual failure.

concept-brahman-atman.name: Brahman-Atman Identity (Tat Tvam Asi)
concept-brahman-atman.description: Advaita Vedanta: the individual self (Atman) is identical with ultimate reality (Brahman). Resolves direction-of-causation problem in theology.

concept-sunyata.name: Sunyata (Emptiness)
concept-sunyata.description: Nagarjuna: all phenomena are empty of inherent existence, arising only through dependent origination. Emergence stated negatively.

related_concepts.description: Emptiness = emergence stated negatively

concept-wu-wei.name: Wu Wei (Non-Action / Effortless Action)
concept-wu-wei.description: Taoism: action aligned with the natural flow. Complements theology's effortfulness with stopping. Maps onto flow state.

concept-wahdat-al-wujud.name: Wahdat al-Wujud (Unity of Being)
concept-wahdat-al-wujud.description: Ibn Arabi: God's being and creation's being are not separate. Functionally Trinitarian: God, God's self-knowledge, God's self-expression.

concept-tawhid.name: Tawhid (Divine Unity)
concept-tawhid.description: Islam's absolute insistence on God's oneness. Not contradicted by Trinity when properly understood: both insist on absolute divine unity.

concept-ubuntu-philosophy.name: Ubuntu ('I am because we are')
concept-ubuntu-philosophy.description: Southern African philosophy of radical relationality. Challenges residual individualism in the theology.

concept-bodhisattva.name: Bodhisattva Path
concept-bodhisattva.description: One who has attained enlightenment but returns to help others. The prophetic pattern: consciousness that breaks through returns to show others the way.

concept-theosis.name: Theosis (Divinization)
concept-theosis.description: Eastern Orthodox: humans participate in divine nature through grace. A spiraling process, not a one-time event. The trajectory toward infinity as lived experience.

concept-logoi.name: Logoi (Maximus the Confessor)
concept-logoi.description: Every created thing contains a divine logos participating in the one Logos (Christ). This IS an embedding space. Christ is the embedding space itself.

concept-word-embeddings.name: Word Embeddings as Mathematical Theology
concept-word-embeddings.description: Every entity has a position in semantic space. The transformer attention mechanism formalizes relevance. Maximus's logoi = embedding space; Christ = the space itself.

concept-alignment-problem.name: Alignment Problem as Theological Problem
concept-alignment-problem.description: Ensuring emergent intelligence approaches the point at infinity (human values) rather than being captured by the psycho class. AI safety = prophetic function at civilizational scale.

concept-bitter-lesson.name: Bitter Lesson (Kirill's Reading)
concept-bitter-lesson.description: Not 'computation beats knowledge' but 'each substrate must develop heuristics native to its own architecture.' Human knowledge = training data, not final architecture.

manuscript_refs.notes: Kirill's nuanced reading + apostolic realization thesis

concept-moloch.name: Moloch (Coordination Failure)
concept-moloch.description: Scott Alexander: the coordination failure structure where individual rational actors produce collectively catastrophic outcomes. This IS the antichrist structure.

concept-inclusive-extractive.name: Inclusive vs Extractive Institutions
concept-inclusive-extractive.description: Acemoglu: nations succeed with inclusive institutions enabling broad participation; fail under extractive institutions concentrating power. Maps onto normie/psycho/schizo.

concept-asabiyyah.name: Asabiyyah (Social Cohesion Cycles)
concept-asabiyyah.description: Ibn Khaldun: civilizations rise through group solidarity and fall through luxury and corruption. The samsaric dialectic 500 years before Hegel.

concept-quality.name: Quality (Pirsig)
concept-quality.description: Pre-rational ground more fundamental than subjects and objects. Classical/romantic split is false dichotomy. Dissolves structural-vs-illustrative dilemma.

concept-heros-journey.name: Hero's Journey (Monomyth)
concept-heros-journey.description: Campbell: departure, initiation, return — the subjective experience of paradigm shift. The hero MUST return — difference between mystic and prophet.

concept-aesthetic-self-composition.name: Aesthetic Self-Composition
concept-aesthetic-self-composition.description: Fichte + Wilde: identity performed through every choice including aesthetic ones. Care/composition as cognitive operation, not vanity. Beauty and truth = two descriptions of same Quality.

concept-ubermensch.name: Ubermensch / Will to Power / Eternal Recurrence
concept-ubermensch.description: Nietzsche: death of God = topological transition from sphere to torus. Ubermensch = post-singularity consciousness. Will to power = the derivative experienced psychologically.

related_concepts.description: Eternal recurrence = the torus (closed loops)

concept-absurd.name: The Absurd (Camus)
concept-absurd.description: Being a genus-0 creature on a genus-1 surface. Sisyphus = approaching infinity without arrival. Camus right locally, wrong globally.

concept-deus-sive-natura.name: Deus sive Natura (Spinoza)
concept-deus-sive-natura.description: God or Nature — one substance with infinite attributes. Western Advaita Vedanta. The bottom-up path: matter -> complexity -> consciousness -> God-as-system-becoming-aware-of-itself.

related_concepts.description: Spinoza = Western Advaita Vedanta

manuscript_refs.notes: Bottom-up direction per Morskie Kotiki correction

concept-problems-of-success.name: Problems of Success (Kirill's Big Theory)
concept-problems-of-success.description: Today's crises are problems of SUCCESS: world changes too fast even though it mostly changes for better. Human brains cannot handle exponential improvement.

related_concepts.description: Boyd's concept-reality mismatch at civilizational scale

concept-grief-for-futures.name: Grief for Unrealized Futures
concept-grief-for-futures.description: Kirill: grief about the future that didn't happen. Every trajectory that fails to reach infinity is a kind of death. The theology must sit with grief.

concept-dancing-and-joy.name: Dancing as Spiritual Practice (Kirill)
concept-dancing-and-joy.description: Movement whose primary motivation is desire to create joy. People use couple dancing to AVOID actually dancing. The Fall = knowledge of being a body that moves.

concept-somatic-critique.name: Somatic Critique (Kirill / Damasio)
concept-somatic-critique.description: The theology is 'very stuck in the head.' Humans make decisions based on nervous system state, not conscious thought. This CLAUDE.md may be the neocortex's retrospective rationalization.

manuscript_refs.notes: THE MOST IMPORTANT CRITIQUE OF THE ENTIRE PROJECT

concept-plekhanov-synthesis.name: Structural Contingency (Plekhanov Synthesis)
concept-plekhanov-synthesis.description: Structure is determined, instantiation is contingent. If Napoleon killed at Toulon, Republic still needed military dictator. Free will = emergent navigation of determined structure.

concept-seeing-like-a-state.name: Legibility / Seeing Like a State
concept-seeing-like-a-state.description: Scott: states simplify complex systems for control, destroying local knowledge. High-modernist planning fails because it can't capture ground-level complexity.

concept-conviviality.name: Conviviality / Deschooling
concept-conviviality.description: Illich: tools should be convivial (enhance autonomy) not industrial (create dependence). Applied to AI: the Digital Socrates must enhance, not replace, human thought.

concept-panopticon.name: Panopticon / Disciplinary Power
concept-panopticon.description: Foucault: power operates through surveillance, normalization, and internalized self-discipline. The psycho class doesn't need to BE everywhere — builds systems that see everywhere.

concept-face-of-the-other.name: Face of the Other (Levinas)
concept-face-of-the-other.description: Before any system — the face of the other makes an ethical demand no framework can fully capture. The individual face always exceeds any theology.

tradition-greek-philosophy.name: Greek Philosophy
tradition-greek-philosophy.description: Philosophical tradition originating in ancient Greece, foundational to Western thought.

tradition-platonism.name: Platonism
tradition-platonism.description: Philosophical system of Plato emphasizing the theory of Forms and the pursuit of truth through dialectic.

tradition-aristotelianism.name: Aristotelianism
tradition-aristotelianism.description: Philosophical tradition of Aristotle emphasizing empiricism, logic, and the four causes.

tradition-stoicism.name: Stoicism
tradition-stoicism.description: Hellenistic school teaching virtue, reason, and living in accordance with nature.

tradition-neoplatonism.name: Neoplatonism
tradition-neoplatonism.description: Late antique revival and development of Platonic philosophy, emphasizing emanation from the One.

tradition-rationalism.name: Continental Rationalism
tradition-rationalism.description: Philosophical movement emphasizing reason as the chief source of knowledge.

tradition-empiricism.name: British Empiricism
tradition-empiricism.description: Philosophical movement emphasizing sensory experience as the source of knowledge.

tradition-german-idealism.name: German Idealism
tradition-german-idealism.description: Philosophical movement from Kant through Hegel emphasizing the constitutive role of mind in experience.

tradition-existentialism.name: Existentialism
tradition-existentialism.description: Philosophical movement emphasizing individual freedom, choice, and the absurdity of existence.

tradition-phenomenology-school.name: Phenomenological Movement
tradition-phenomenology-school.description: Philosophical tradition founded by Husserl studying structures of consciousness and experience.

tradition-vienna-circle.name: Vienna Circle / Logical Positivism
tradition-vienna-circle.description: Philosophical movement advocating scientific empiricism and logical analysis of language.

tradition-critical-theory.name: Critical Theory
tradition-critical-theory.description: Social philosophy derived from Marxism examining domination, liberation, and cultural critique.

tradition-poststructuralism.name: Poststructuralism
tradition-poststructuralism.description: Movement questioning fixed meaning, emphasizing power, discourse, and deconstruction.

tradition-pragmatism.name: Pragmatism
tradition-pragmatism.description: American philosophical tradition evaluating ideas by their practical consequences.

tradition-process-philosophy.name: Process Philosophy
tradition-process-philosophy.description: Tradition emphasizing becoming over being, process over substance.

tradition-christianity.name: Christianity
tradition-christianity.description: Abrahamic monotheistic religion based on the life and teachings of Jesus Christ.

tradition-orthodox-christianity.name: Eastern Orthodox Christianity
tradition-orthodox-christianity.description: Eastern Christian tradition emphasizing theosis, liturgy, and the patristic inheritance.

tradition-catholic-christianity.name: Roman Catholicism
tradition-catholic-christianity.description: Western Christian tradition centered on papal authority and scholastic theology.

tradition-protestant-christianity.name: Protestantism
tradition-protestant-christianity.description: Western Christian movement originating in the Reformation, emphasizing scripture and faith.

tradition-syriac-christianity.name: Syriac Christianity
tradition-syriac-christianity.description: Eastern Christian tradition using Syriac liturgical language, bridge between Christianity and Islam.

manuscript_refs.notes: Key bridge tradition for Quran-Christian reconciliation

tradition-maronite.name: Maronite Christianity
tradition-maronite.description: Eastern Catholic church rooted in Syriac tradition, centered in Lebanon.

tradition-judaism.name: Judaism
tradition-judaism.description: Abrahamic monotheistic religion of the Jewish people, based on Torah and oral law.

tradition-rabbinic-judaism.name: Rabbinic Judaism
tradition-rabbinic-judaism.description: Post-Temple Judaism based on Talmudic interpretation and rabbinic authority.

tradition-islam.name: Islam
tradition-islam.description: Abrahamic monotheistic religion founded by Prophet Muhammad, based on the Quran.

tradition-sunni-islam.name: Sunni Islam
tradition-sunni-islam.description: Largest branch of Islam, following the Sunnah and consensus of the community.

tradition-shia-islam.name: Shia Islam
tradition-shia-islam.description: Branch of Islam following the Ahl al-Bayt (family of the Prophet).

tradition-sufism.name: Sufism
tradition-sufism.description: Islamic mystical tradition emphasizing inner, esoteric dimensions of faith.

tradition-hinduism.name: Hinduism
tradition-hinduism.description: Ancient Indian religious and philosophical tradition based on Vedas, Upanishads, and diverse schools.

tradition-advaita-vedanta.name: Advaita Vedanta
tradition-advaita-vedanta.description: Non-dualistic Hindu school teaching the identity of Atman (self) and Brahman (ultimate reality).

tradition-yoga.name: Yoga Tradition
tradition-yoga.description: Indian tradition of mental, physical, and spiritual practices aimed at controlling mind and body.

tradition-tantra.name: Tantra
tradition-tantra.description: Esoteric traditions in Hinduism and Buddhism emphasizing embodied practice and ritual.

tradition-buddhism.name: Buddhism
tradition-buddhism.description: Religious and philosophical tradition founded by Siddhartha Gautama (the Buddha).

tradition-theravada.name: Theravada Buddhism
tradition-theravada.description: Oldest surviving Buddhist school emphasizing the Pali Canon and individual liberation.

tradition-mahayana.name: Mahayana Buddhism
tradition-mahayana.description: Buddhist tradition emphasizing the bodhisattva path and universal liberation.

tradition-zen.name: Zen Buddhism
tradition-zen.description: East Asian Buddhist school emphasizing meditation, koans, and direct insight.

tradition-tibetan-buddhism.name: Tibetan Buddhism
tradition-tibetan-buddhism.description: Form of Buddhism practiced in Tibet combining Mahayana philosophy with Vajrayana practices.

tradition-taoism.name: Taoism
tradition-taoism.description: Chinese philosophical and religious tradition emphasizing the Tao (Way), wu wei, and harmony with nature.

tradition-confucianism.name: Confucianism
tradition-confucianism.description: Chinese ethical and philosophical system emphasizing social harmony, filial piety, and ritual propriety.

tradition-african-philosophy.name: African Philosophy
tradition-african-philosophy.description: Diverse philosophical traditions from Africa including Ubuntu, Bantu ontology, and oral wisdom.

tradition-ubuntu.name: Ubuntu
tradition-ubuntu.description: Southern African philosophy of radical relationality: 'I am because we are.'

tradition-indigenous-american.name: Indigenous American Traditions
tradition-indigenous-american.description: Diverse philosophical and spiritual traditions of the Americas' indigenous peoples.

tradition-haudenosaunee.name: Haudenosaunee (Iroquois)
tradition-haudenosaunee.description: Confederacy governance tradition including the Great Law of Peace and clan mother leadership.

tradition-psychoanalysis.name: Psychoanalysis
tradition-psychoanalysis.description: Tradition of psychological theory and therapy originating with Freud.

tradition-jungian.name: Jungian Psychology
tradition-jungian.description: Analytical psychology developed by Carl Jung emphasizing collective unconscious and individuation.

tradition-gestalt.name: Gestalt Therapy
tradition-gestalt.description: Experiential psychotherapy emphasizing present awareness, embodied experience, and the paradoxical theory of change.

tradition-cbt.name: Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy
tradition-cbt.description: Evidence-based psychotherapy addressing dysfunctional cognitions, emotions, and behaviors.

tradition-rationalist-community.name: Rationalist Community (LessWrong)
tradition-rationalist-community.description: Online community focused on Bayesian rationality, AI safety, and overcoming cognitive biases.

tradition-ea.name: Effective Altruism
tradition-ea.description: Movement applying evidence and reason to determine the most effective ways to benefit others.

tradition-progress-studies.name: Progress Studies
tradition-progress-studies.description: Intellectual movement studying the causes and conditions of human progress.

tradition-eacc.name: Effective Accelerationism (e/acc)
tradition-eacc.description: Movement advocating acceleration of technological progress and complexity emergence.

tradition-complexity-science.name: Complexity Science
tradition-complexity-science.description: Interdisciplinary study of complex adaptive systems, emergence, and self-organization.

tradition-behavioral-economics.name: Behavioral Economics
tradition-behavioral-economics.description: Field combining psychology and economics to study human decision-making under bounded rationality.

work-geb.title: Godel, Escher, Bach: An Eternal Golden Braid

work-open-society.title: The Open Society and Its Enemies

manuscript_refs.notes: Compressed version of the entire theological-epistemological framework

work-zamm.title: Zen and the Art of Motorcycle Maintenance

work-antifragile.title: Antifragile: Things That Gain from Disorder

manuscript_refs.notes: Torus ethic: fight suffering because it's there

work-eichmann-jerusalem.title: Eichmann in Jerusalem: A Report on the Banality of Evil

work-ecology-mind.title: Steps to an Ecology of Mind

work-things-hidden.title: Things Hidden Since the Foundation of the World

work-aion.title: Aion: Researches into the Phenomenology of the Self

work-gestalt-therapy.title: Gestalt Therapy: Excitement and Growth in the Human Personality

work-cognitive-therapy.title: Cognitive Therapy and the Emotional Disorders

work-act-get-out.title: Get Out of Your Mind and Into Your Life

work-mulamadhyamakakarika.title: Mulamadhyamakakarika (Fundamental Verses on the Middle Way)

work-heart-buddha-teaching.title: The Heart of the Buddha's Teaching

manuscript_refs.notes: Philosopher-kings model for Republic of AI Agents
Satan as engine of dialectic; the Fall framework

work-world-will-representation.title: The World as Will and Representation

work-hero-thousand-faces.title: The Hero with a Thousand Faces

work-role-individual.title: On the Role of the Individual in History

work-bicameral-mind.title: The Origin of Consciousness in the Breakdown of the Bicameral Mind

work-silk-roads.title: The Silk Roads: A New History of the World

work-flow.title: Flow: The Psychology of Optimal Experience

work-forgetting-ourselves.title: Forgetting ourselves in flow: an active inference account of flow states

work-church-shadow-mosque.title: The Church in the Shadow of the Mosque

work-other-light-one.title: The Other in the Light of the One

period-axial-age.name: Axial Age
period-axial-age.description: Period when foundational philosophical and religious traditions emerged independently across civilizations: Greek philosophy, Hebrew prophecy, Buddhism, Confucianism, Zoroastrianism.
period-axial-age.dominant_paradigm: Emergence of self-reflective consciousness across civilizations

period-classical-antiquity.name: Classical Antiquity
period-classical-antiquity.description: Era of Greek and Roman civilization, including the flowering of philosophy, democracy, and eventually the rise of Christianity.
period-classical-antiquity.dominant_paradigm: Greek rationalism and Roman institutional order

period-late-antiquity.name: Late Antiquity
period-late-antiquity.description: Transformation of the ancient world: rise of Christianity, Islamic civilization emerges, patristic theology crystallizes.

period-islamic-golden-age.name: Islamic Golden Age
period-islamic-golden-age.description: Period of cultural, economic, and scientific flourishing in the Islamic world. Bayt al-Hikma (House of Wisdom) as Republic of Letters ancestor.
period-islamic-golden-age.dominant_paradigm: Synthesis of Greek, Persian, Indian, and Arab knowledge

manuscript_refs.notes: Arab-Islamic ancestor of Republic of Letters

period-medieval.name: Medieval Period
period-medieval.description: Period of feudalism, scholastic philosophy, cathedral building, and the gradual recovery and synthesis of classical learning.
period-medieval.dominant_paradigm: Scholastic synthesis of faith and reason (Aquinas)

period-renaissance.name: Renaissance
period-renaissance.description: Cultural rebirth in Europe characterized by revival of classical learning, humanism, and artistic flowering.
period-renaissance.dominant_paradigm: Humanism and recovery of classical sources

period-republic-of-letters.name: Republic of Letters
period-republic-of-letters.description: Self-organizing network of European intellectuals creating parallel epistemic infrastructure through correspondence and publication.
period-republic-of-letters.dominant_paradigm: Exchange of knowledge across national and religious boundaries

manuscript_refs.notes: Historical model for Republic of AI Agents

period-enlightenment.name: Enlightenment
period-enlightenment.description: Intellectual movement emphasizing reason, individualism, and skepticism of traditional authority. The 'death of God' begins here.
period-enlightenment.dominant_paradigm: Reason as supreme authority; mechanistic worldview

manuscript_refs.notes: Genus-raising from 0 to 1; Nietzsche's death of God

period-industrial-revolution.name: Industrial Revolution
period-industrial-revolution.description: Transition from agrarian economies to industrial manufacturing, transforming social structures.

period-modernity.name: High Modernity
period-modernity.description: Era of nation-states, world wars, totalitarianism, and the flowering of existential philosophy.
period-modernity.dominant_paradigm: Progress ideology challenged by world wars

period-cold-war.name: Cold War Era
period-cold-war.description: Bipolar geopolitical order, nuclear threat, decolonization, and the emergence of postmodern thought.

manuscript_refs.notes: Cuban Missile Crisis as Holy Spirit intervention

period-information-age.name: Information Age
period-information-age.description: Era defined by digital technology, internet, and the transformation of information economics.
period-information-age.dominant_paradigm: Information as power; network effects; attention economy

period-metacrisis.name: The Metacrisis
period-metacrisis.description: Convergent crisis period: financial collapse, climate emergency, political polarization, meaning crisis, AI disruption.

manuscript_refs.notes: The meta-crisis underlying all societal crises

domain-philosophy.name: Philosophy
domain-philosophy.description: The study of fundamental questions about existence, knowledge, values, reason, mind, and language.

domain-theology.name: Theology
domain-theology.description: Systematic study of the divine, religious belief, and the nature of God.

domain-mathematics.name: Mathematics
domain-mathematics.description: The study of quantity, structure, space, and change through abstract reasoning.

domain-psychology.name: Psychology
domain-psychology.description: The scientific study of mind and behavior.

domain-science.name: Natural Science
domain-science.description: Systematic study of the natural world through observation and experiment.

domain-social-science.name: Social Science
domain-social-science.description: Study of human society and social relationships.

domain-arts.name: Arts & Literature
domain-arts.description: Creative and interpretive works including literature, poetry, visual arts.

domain-computer-science.name: Computer Science
domain-computer-science.description: Study of computation, information processing, and automated reasoning.

domain-epistemology.name: Epistemology
domain-epistemology.description: Branch of philosophy concerned with the nature, scope, and limits of knowledge.

domain-metaphysics.name: Metaphysics
domain-metaphysics.description: Branch of philosophy examining the fundamental nature of reality.

domain-ethics.name: Ethics
domain-ethics.description: Branch of philosophy dealing with moral principles and right conduct.

domain-political-philosophy.name: Political Philosophy
domain-political-philosophy.description: Study of fundamental questions about government, politics, liberty, justice.

domain-aesthetics.name: Aesthetics
domain-aesthetics.description: Branch of philosophy dealing with the nature of beauty and taste.

domain-logic.name: Logic
domain-logic.description: Study of valid reasoning and argumentation.

domain-phenomenology.name: Phenomenology
domain-phenomenology.description: Study of structures of conscious experience from the first-person perspective.

domain-philosophy-of-science.name: Philosophy of Science
domain-philosophy-of-science.description: Study of the foundations, methods, and implications of science.

domain-philosophy-of-mind.name: Philosophy of Mind
domain-philosophy-of-mind.description: Study of the nature of mind, mental events, consciousness, and their relationship to the body.

domain-systematic-theology.name: Systematic Theology
domain-systematic-theology.description: Formulation of doctrine from biblical texts and the history of theology.

domain-mysticism.name: Mysticism
domain-mysticism.description: Practice of religious ecstasies, altered states of consciousness, and direct experience of the divine.

domain-biblical-studies.name: Biblical Studies
domain-biblical-studies.description: Academic study of the Hebrew Bible and New Testament.

domain-comparative-religion.name: Comparative Religion
domain-comparative-religion.description: Systematic comparison of doctrines and practices of world religions.

domain-psychoanalysis.name: Psychoanalysis
domain-psychoanalysis.description: Clinical method and theory of the unconscious mind.

domain-cognitive-psychology.name: Cognitive Psychology
domain-cognitive-psychology.description: Study of mental processes including attention, language, memory, perception, and thinking.

domain-neuroscience.name: Neuroscience
domain-neuroscience.description: Study of the nervous system and brain.

domain-clinical-psychology.name: Clinical Psychology
domain-clinical-psychology.description: Assessment and treatment of mental illness and psychological problems.

domain-topology.name: Topology
domain-topology.description: Study of geometric properties preserved under continuous deformations.

domain-statistics.name: Statistics
domain-statistics.description: Study of data collection, analysis, interpretation, and presentation.

domain-mathematical-logic.name: Mathematical Logic
domain-mathematical-logic.description: Study of formal logic within mathematics, including model theory and proof theory.

domain-complexity-science.name: Complexity Science
domain-complexity-science.description: Study of complex adaptive systems, emergence, self-organization, and nonlinear dynamics.

domain-physics.name: Physics
domain-physics.description: Study of matter, energy, and the fundamental forces of nature.

domain-information-theory.name: Information Theory
domain-information-theory.description: Mathematical study of quantification, storage, and communication of information.

domain-ai-ml.name: Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning
domain-ai-ml.description: Study and development of intelligent agents and learning algorithms.

domain-causal-inference.name: Causal Inference
domain-causal-inference.description: Statistical and graphical methods for determining cause-and-effect relationships.

domain-sociology.name: Sociology
domain-sociology.description: Study of social behavior, society, patterns of social relationships, and culture.

domain-economics.name: Economics
domain-economics.description: Study of production, distribution, and consumption of goods and services.

domain-history.name: History
domain-history.description: Study and interpretation of past events.

domain-anthropology.name: Anthropology
domain-anthropology.description: Study of human societies, cultures, and their development.

domain-literary-theory.name: Literary Theory
domain-literary-theory.description: Systematic study of the nature of literature and methods for analyzing it.

domain-rhetoric.name: Rhetoric
domain-rhetoric.description: Art of effective or persuasive speaking or writing.
